_comment: council config. A 'profile' is a POLICY (recipe, code_access, panel_size); the concrete roster is resolved at runtime from the available agents. Reached as 'consult council'.
_max_embedded_lines_doc: Total lines of code embedded into the council question. Extra files beyond this budget are omitted with a visible [TRUNCATED] marker.
_max_synth_chars_doc: Per-member answer cap fed into the synthesizer. Answers beyond this are truncated with a visible marker.
_strip_patterns_doc: Regexes matched at the START of an answer to strip boilerplate that advisor agents prepend because of this machine's global agent instructions (activation token + SSoT line). Set to [] on a clean environment.
strip_patterns: Query: You are a peer AI advisor[^\n]*
You are a peer AI advisor[^\n]*
_profiles_doc: Profiles are POLICIES, not fixed rosters. recipe + code_access + panel_size only; the concrete panel/synth are resolved at runtime by roster.py from whatever agents `consult --list` reports installed, with cross-provider diversity. Fewer available agents = smaller council (down to single-agent), never a crash. To pin a roster, add explicit panel/synthesizer/drafter/reviewers and it is used as-is.

consilium-verify._doc: Draft -> adversarial review -> synthesize. POLICY: roster resolved at runtime from available code-reader agents (degrades to parallel if <2 answerers). For real code audits prefer `consult council` (orchestrator embeds code as text).

consilium-budget._doc: Parallel independent answers + synthesis, code_access=false. The profile `consult council` uses: code is embedded in the question as text, so any installed agent can read it. POLICY: roster resolved from all available agents.

consilium-frontier._doc: Strongest readers, parallel + synthesis, code_access=true via --code. POLICY: roster resolved at runtime, restricted to code-reader agents (agy/claude/codex).
